obverse.description:
Independence Memorial Hall, Colombo

Various progress in 50 years of independence: Free Education & Health Services, Gal Oya Development Project, Electricity Development, Bandaranaike Memorial International Conference Hall, Mahaweli Development Project, Bandaranaike International Airport, Telecommunications Development, Investment Promotion Zone, New Parliament Complex in Kotte, Industrial Development, Development of the Colombo City, Religious harmony and peace showing a Church, Mosque, Temple, Pagoda and the unity of the people.
obverse.lettering:
ශ්‍රී ලංකාණ්ඩුව වෙනුවෙන් නිකුත් කරන ලද මේ
මුදල් නෝට්ටුව ශ්‍රී ලංකාව ඇතුළත ඕනෑම මුදල් ගණනක්
ගෙවිම සඳහා නිතියෙන් වලංගුය 

1998-02-04

ශ්‍රී ලංකා මහ බැංකුව
இலங்கை மத்திய வங்கி
CENTRAL BANK OF SRI LANKA

රුපියල් දෙසියයි
இருநூறு ரூபாய்
TWO HUNDRED RUPEES

නිදහස  சுதந்திரம்  Independence 1948-1998
obverse.lettering_translation:
This banknote issued on behalf of the Government of Sri Lanka is legally valid for payment of any amount within Sri Lanka

1998-02-04

CENTRAL BANK OF SRI LANKA

TWO HUNDRED RUPEES

Independence 1948-1998

reverse.description:
Temple of the Tooth, Kandy

Various historical scenes: Advent of Prince Vijaya, Arrival of Arahant Mahinda and introduction of Buddhism, King Dutugemunu unites Sri Lanka, Construction of Sigiriya Rock Fortress, Construction of Sea of Parakrama, European powers colonizing Sri Lanka, Wariyapola Sri Sumangala Thero hauling down the British flag in 1815
reverse.lettering:
ශ්‍රී ලංකා මහ බැංකුව
இலங்கை மத்திய வங்கி
CENTRAL BANK OF SRI LANKA

රුපියල් දෙසියයි
இருநூறு ரூபாய்
TWO HUNDRED RUPEES

watermark.description: Lion holding sword in front of number '200'
